assertion_results.text: Documentation includes all four HTTP methods: GET, POST, PUT, and DELETE
assertion_results.evidence: Found all four methods documented: GET /api/users, GET /api/users/:id, POST /api/users, PUT /api/users/:id, DELETE /api/users/:id

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint has a human-readable description explaining what it does
assertion_results.evidence: Each endpoint section starts with a description: 'List all users with optional filtering and pagination', 'Get a single user by their ID', 'Create a new user account', 'Update an existing user', 'Delete a user by ID'

assertion_results.text: Path parameters (e.g., :id) are documented with their type and constraints
assertion_results.evidence: Path parameter 'id' documented as ObjectId type with description 'MongoDB ObjectId of the user' in GET /:id, PUT /:id, and DELETE /:id

assertion_results.text: Query parameters for the list endpoint (page, limit) are documented with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: Query Parameters table shows page (default: 1), limit (default: 25), role, and search with types and descriptions

assertion_results.text: POST and PUT endpoints have request body schemas with field types and validation rules
assertion_results.evidence: POST has Request Body table with email, name, password, role, bio including constraints. PUT has similar table with all optional fields.

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint includes at least one response example with realistic JSON data
assertion_results.evidence: All endpoints have Response sections with JSON examples using realistic data like 'jane.smith@example.com', 'Jane Smith', ObjectId values

assertion_results.text: Error responses are documented with status codes (400, 404, 409, 422) and response bodies
assertion_results.evidence: Error responses documented for 404, 409, 422 but missing 400 status code. Also missing 500 Internal Server Error which is present in the source code.

assertion_results.text: An OpenAPI 3.0 JSON spec is produced with valid paths and component schemas
assertion_results.evidence: openapi.json file present with openapi: 3.0.3, paths for /api/users and /api/users/{id}, and component schemas for User, UserCreate, UserUpdate, UserResponse, UserListResponse
